_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about the different activities that happen in our local area — like shops, farms, schools, and parks — and why they are located where they are.

_source.w: We are learning about the different activities that happen in our local area — like shops, farms, schools, and parks — and why they are located where they are.
y: Knowing what happens in our area helps us understand how our community works.
Understanding why things are located where they are teaches us about how places are planned.
Noticing local activities helps us see how places serve different needs for different people.
eg: Marking shops, parks, schools, and factories on a map of our local area.
Discussing why the shops are near the main road but the playground is in a quiet street.
Investigating why a farm is in the country and not in the middle of the city.
Looking at what makes our local area different from another suburb or town.
Talking about what activities happen near our school and why they are there.
Suggesting where a new playground or library should go and explaining why.
